claim_verdicts.description: Checks whether full internal RL stays ahead of the strongest control with retained acceptance gates and statistical evidence.
Checks whether the internal-RL advantage is backed by sparse or delayed reward evidence rather than shaping leakage.
Checks whether temporal-abstraction evidence survives matched scaffold ablations instead of relying on a fast-prior shortcut.
Checks whether delayed and long-horizon NL signals reach temporal families and fast-prior evidence.
claim_verdicts.summary: NL slow-loop support for ETA fast path claim verdict.

claim_verdicts.description: Checks whether ETA evidence uses real open-weight residual capture/control rather than only synthetic trace proof harness evidence.
claim_verdicts.summary: ETA real open-weight residual-control claim verdict.

description: Unified ETA evidence bundle for eta-open-weight-paper-suite-small.

artifact_expectations: fail-closed fallback and hook-coverage thresholds for real residual claims
gate semantics marker for artifact freshness checks
manifest.description: Open-weight ETA paper suite paper-suite-small using real transformer residual capture/control as the primary backend and trace as the matched fallback control.

primary_metrics.description: Held-out terminal success rate for the full internal-RL profile.
Held-out strong sparse-reward success rate for the full internal-RL profile.
Held-out abstract-action reuse rate for the full internal-RL profile.
Held-out delayed-credit alignment for the full internal-RL profile.
Held-out strong success gap between the full profile and the strongest matched control.
Gap in held-out strong success across backends for the full profile.
Composite real-residual policy/control advantage over same-backend matched controls, excluding backend-ablation noops.

secondary_metrics.description: Fraction of ETA acceptance gates passed in the assessment report.
Observed policy parameter update rate for the full profile.
Held-out subgoal completion rate for the full profile.
Held-out strong success variability across held-out cases.
Average rollout count consumed by each full-profile update.
Average parameter-change norm for full-profile internal RL updates.
Average replacement-effect delta observed during full-profile updates.
Average critic/value loss for the full internal-RL profile.
Total training transitions consumed by full-profile internal RL updates.
Mean rollout steps controlled by one abstract action before switching.
Fraction of abstract-action windows persisting for at least two rollout steps.
Residual-control signal quality combining downstream effect and backend fidelity.
Number of temporal families receiving long-horizon delayed-credit writes.
Fraction of temporal families with payoff or delayed-credit evidence.
Fast-prior benefit retained over the no-fast-prior control.
Fraction of reward signal not attributable to shaping leakage.
Share of optimizer-visible reward coming from shaping components.
Held-out family reuse retained over the no-fast-prior scaffold ablation.
Held-out delayed-credit assignments whose abstract-action window lacks an assigned family.
Held-out terminal successes backed by terminal delayed-credit assignments.
Fraction of ETA substrate steps captured from a real open-weight runtime.
Mean actual hook fire rate observed during real open-weight ETA capture; real residual claims require >= 0.75.
Planned selected hook layers divided by total transformer blocks; context only, not a hard hook-health gate.
Fraction of captured token positions that expose residual activations across the selected hook layers.
Fraction of real open-weight snapshots carrying a nonempty residual sequence.
Fraction of real open-weight snapshots using prefix-aligned before/after intervention protocol.
Fraction of real ETA capture served by a builtin fallback runtime; real residual claims require <= 0.00.
Mean residual intervention application count in ETA open-weight proof rollouts.
Mean per-episode replacement-effect delta for real residual-control rollouts.

gates.description: Full internal RL should beat matched controls on held-out terminal success and strong sparse-reward success, or win tied sparse-reward outcomes with stronger mechanism evidence.
Discovered abstract-action families should be reused across cases, not recreated every time.
Held-out subgoal recombinations should remain solvable with non-trivial completion.
Delayed sparse reward should align with the abstract-action windows that preceded success.
Internal RL should show concrete parameter-update evidence during training, not only better outcomes.
Stronger ETA claims require batch-level rollout evidence, non-trivial replacement effect, and bounded held-out variance.
The proof should persist across trace and synthetic-open-weight backends.
